title: Staggered Leaky Windbreaks Against the Steppe Gale
mutation: input: a lattice advection-diffusion solver over W columns x H lanes replaces a static blocking model -- planted trees only ever RELOCATE momentum (drag removes it locally, but a permille split sends part of it as an immediate jet onto the two nearest lanes, mirrored back at the plantable band's true edges, and the rest into a wider 5-lane diffusion pool one column downwind), coupled to a rational crosswind drift that rotates the whole lane vector downwind and a per-cell porosity-level menu (cost/drag/jet all increasing with density) the solver must budget across a staggered footprint.
strategies: trivial: plant nothing (reproduces the checker's own do-nothing baseline)
naive greedy: build the densest wall you can afford in the very first column, prioritizing the raw-windiest lanes, ignoring crop locations, drift, and tip-jet effects entirely

insight (strong): simulate the exact conservation-law mechanics and rank every (column,lane,porosity) placement by simulated marginal score-per-cost, then greedily fill the budget and locally refine -- this consistently prefers several cheap leaky plantings staggered across columns over one expensive solid wall, because a sum-of-squares objective punishes concentrated jets harder than thinly spread relief

a general solver can go further: jointly optimize placement + drift compensation + tip-avoidance with proper search (simulated annealing / ILP relaxation) instead of the marginal-then-refine heuristic
